titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Margaret A. Farley papers
titlestmt.author: Divinity Library staff

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Monday March 17, 2025 at 18:19

did.abstract: The Margaret Farley Papers include materials relating to Farley's career as a Christian Ethics professor and her research, writings, and course materials relating to subject fields such as sexual ethics, history of Christian ethics, medical ethics, and more.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The collection is arranged in six series:

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Margaret Farley, born on April 15, 1935, is a renowned authority in the areas of the history of ethics, feminist ethics, phenomenology and ethics, medical ethics, sexual ethics, ethics and spirituality, and responses to the AIDS pandemic in Africa. She is Gilbert L. Stark Professor Emerita of Christian Ethics at Yale Divinity School, where she taught from 1971 until 2007. She earned her B.A. and M.A. degrees from the University of Detroit before coming to Yale, where she received the M.Phil degree in 1970 and her Ph.D. in 1973. Farley is a member of the Catholic Sisters of Mercy and a previous president of the Catholic Theological Society of America. She was one of the first Catholic faculty members at Yale Divinity School and the first woman to receive tenure. Her accolades include receiving the John Courtney Murray Award from the Catholic Theological Society of America in 1992, serving as president of the Society of Christian Ethics, the University of Louisville Grawemeyer Award in Religion in 2008, and honorary doctorates from numerous colleges and universities. A Festschrift in honor of Farley A Just and True Love: Feminism at the Frontiers of Theological Ethics , edited by Maura A. Ryan and Brian F. Linnane was published in 2007. Though Farley retired in 2007, her research continues.

Farley's most recent books include Compassionate Respect: A Feminist Approach to Medical Ethics. Madeleva Lecture. (Paulist Press, 2002), Just Love: A Framework for Christian Sexual Ethics (Continuum 2006), and Changing the Questions: Explorations in Christian Ethics (Orbis, 2015). Her work Just Love was denounced by the Vatican's doctrinal office in 2012. Documentation related to this controversy is included in her papers. In addition to her extensive publishing of articles and books, Farley has served on the editorial boards of journals and served as Co-director of the Yale Interdisciplinary Bioethics Center 1998-2007, Director of Yale Divinity School Women's Initiative on Gender, Faith, and Responses to HIV/AIDS in Africa from 2001-2007, and Co-Director of the All Africa Conference: Sister to Sister (Roman Catholic Women and Responses to HIV/AIDS in Africa), 2001- 2016. She has served on numerous ethics committees, including for Yale-New Haven Hospital, the American College of Obstetricians and Gynecologists, and the American Society for Reproductive Medicine. For a full listing of Farley's publications, awards, and activities, see the CVs in Series VI, Biographical Documentation.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Margaret Farley papers (RG 29), Special Collections, Yale Divinity Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Margaret Farley papers include materials related to courses she taught, committee work on stem cell research, in-vitro fertilization, sex-selection and other topics, writings, presentations, subject files, and biographical documentation. There is correspondence interspersed among the different series; letters related to publications, events, projects, etc. are included with the other available documentation.The organization of this collection benefitted greatly from the work of Prof. Farley's assistant Jamie Manson over the past several years.

The Course Materials of Series I include materials on Feminist Theological Ethics, History of Christian Theological Ethics, Sexual Ethics, Environmental Ethics, Medical Ethics, among others. Series II documents Farley's involvement in various committees and projects. The Writings of Series III, arranged in chronological order, provide an overview of her interests and areas of expertise. The Presentations/Lecture/Talks of Series IV are arranged by title. Resources accumulated by Farley in the course of her research, writing, and teaching are available in Series V, divided into three categories: an alphabetical sequence of topical files arranged by Farley, an alphabetical file containing resources on specific theologians, and a miscellaneous category that contains materials received with the collection that Farley had not arranged in a specific order.

Topics documented by this collections are wide-ranging, from the Georgetown University homosexual group controversy to pornography, hookup culture, views on homosexual clergy and many aspects of sexual and medical ethics. The research materials include opinions from various points of view, and serve as a general inventory of conversations covering controversial topics over the course of several decades.

corpname.part: United States. National Bioethics Advisory Commission
American College of Obstetricians and Gynecologists

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Preparatory notes for the course and handwritten notes and outlines for the first session.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Articles used as preparation for the course and used as introductory material in the first session.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes readings on women and biblical interpretation, and feminist readings of historical texts including biblical material.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This session explores images of God, theory of 'images', and the relationship between each and human experience as a source for feminist theology and ethics.

did.unittitle: Session 8: The Church and Communities of Faith
Session 12: Spirituality and Justice and Course Conclusion
Articles from the Journal of Feminist Studies in Religion; minority voices
Ethical issues in the women's movement
Pastoral documents on women; Toronto feminist theology
Ethical issues in the women's movement
YDS women; agenda for women in the church
Agenda for women in the church

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes book reserve lists and course reading material.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes timelines for the lives of and general bibliography for St. Augustine, Thomas Aquinas, Martin Luther, and John Calvin including both primary and secondary sources.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Outlines for Sessions 8,9,10,11,12,14,17 on Augustine, Aquinas, Luther, and Calvin

did.unittitle: St. Augustine - On The Notion of Time
St. Augustine to Thomas Aquinas - Transition Lecture Notes
Thomas Aquinas and Human Action Notes
John Calvin - Ethics of Calvinism Notes and Readings

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes course wrap-up notes, information on final papers and the final exam, and final exam materials.

did.unittitle: Wegner, The Illusions of the Conscious Will

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Syllabi included for the following semesters: Fall 1974, Fall 1982, Spring 1986, Fall 1988, Fall 1995, Fall 2000, Fall 2002, and Fall 2005.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A collection of reading lists and book selections selected for the course. Bibliographies vary topically, but some of them pulled from other courses such as a Yale Divinity Spring 1974 course on Prayer: Religious Practice and Religious Experience and a Harvard Divinity School Ethics Colloquium on Normative Theory.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Articles in this file are in order of publication date.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Articles in this file are undated, and therefore in no particular order.

did.unittitle: Course Reading Materials on Women and Feminism
Session 2: Meanings of Spirituality in Human History

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This file includes handwritten notes, articles, etc.

did.unittitle: Session 4: Notes on Virtue and Moral Development

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This file includes outlines, annotations, and notes written by Margaret Farley in preparation for this session.

did.unittitle: Session 4: Readings on Virtue and Moral Development

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This file includes articles and readings used in preparation for and assigned for this session.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This file includes both articles and notes pertaining to only the first half of this session, the portion on Mysticism. Some outlines are labeled Session 4, but its placement in the session 5 folder and content allow it to remain.

did.unittitle: Session 5: Notes on Moral and Spiritual Call, Scripture, Prayer

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This file includes notes and handouts pertaining to the second half of this session, the portion on moral and spiritual call, scripture, and prayer.

did.unittitle: Session 5: Readings on Moral and Spiritual Call, Scripture, Prayer

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This file includes articles and reading material relevant for the second half of this session, the portion on moral and spiritual call, scripture, and prayer.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Some outlines say Session 8, but before 2005 all this content was part of session 9, therefore materials notated this way remain in the session 9 file with other related content.

did.unittitle: The Relation Between Christian Morality and Spiritual Life
Syllabi, book reserve lists, course evaluations
Roman Catholic ecclesiology in ecumenical perspective
Roman Catholic ecclesiology in ecumenical perspective
Roman Catholic tradition of social ethics; current issues in ecclesiology
Faith vs. reason; ad tuendam fidem

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes article published in the Yale Bulletin about the course entitled, "Project Boosts Interdisciplinary Debate about Bioethical Issues"

did.unittitle: Session 10: Potential for Harm and Violence
Sessions 11 and 12: Constructing a Sexual Ethic

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: On sexual violence, sexual harassment, pornography, abortion, homosexuality and lesbianism, and general sexual ethics both generally and in relation to the church and religion

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This file includes mainly handwritten notes

did.unittitle: Research on Gender Difference and Roles

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Articles include writings on gender difference in behavior, lesbianism, and feminist perspectives

did.unittitle: Research on Pornography and Sex Work

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a Spring 1994 pornography specific sexual ethics syllabus offered at an unknown institution, and likely taught by someone other than Margaret Farley. Readings predominantly feature female and feminist point of views on the sex industry, including prostitution and pornography. Newspaper and magazine clippings are international, including perspectives from Japan and Thailand. Book lists specific to these subjects are also in this file. Correspondence in regard to these subjects are included.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes an email including a July/August 2008 newsletter published online by The Center for Lesbian and Gay Studies in Religion and Ministry (CLGS) out of Pacific School and Religion in Berkley, CA.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Historical information, modern articles, and personal notes by Margaret Farley included

did.unittitle: Research on Domestic and Sexual Violence

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials include resource guides for domestic and sexual violence, book lists on domestic violence, and articles and notes about both domestic and sexual violence. Be aware that the content in this file addresses incest, sexual abuse, rape as a weapon of war, rape in the form of "military comfort women," and female circumcision (also known as Female Genital Mutilation, FGM).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This file includes bibliographies, case studies, and discussion questions.

did.unittitle: Research on Social Views of Homosexuality

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Writings concerning social, philosophical, and non-scientific academic opinions on homosexuality and lesbianism. Religion and spirituality are relevant topics in many of these articles.

did.unittitle: Research on Homosexuality and the Clergy

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Articles concerning ordination of homosexual persons with many personal testimonials and church responses and opinions.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: In the 1970's, a Catholic bishop named John McNeill came out as gay. His story, and his position within the church, sparked debate and conflict that resulted in Vatican involvement and international outcry. Newspapers and articles written about and by John McNeill concerning his position in the church and the position of the church regarding McNeill are included.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Articles and newspaper clippings concerning political matters and homosexuality including on civil rights, marriage, and military involvement.

did.unittitle: Research Case: Georgetown Ban on Homosexual Groups

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Articles and opinions related to the Gay Rights Coalition student group vs. Georgetown University lawsuit. In the early 1980's, Georgetown University in conjunction with the Roman Catholic Church banned two student groups related to homosexuality. Following the event, the issue resulted in 8 years of lawsuits and made national news. In 1987, the groups were granted equal treatment but not official recognition by the university allowing their use of facilities and granted financing like other student groups. A letter from alumni to Georgetown from April of 1988 is the last article included in this file.

did.unittitle: Bibliographies and Case Studies on Sexual Harassment

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Farley did research and work on sexual harassment policy for Yale University and the topic blends into her sexual ethics course.

did.unittitle: Law and Opinion on Sexual Harassment
Definitions and Policy Creation Regarding Sexual Harassment
Research on Sexual Harassment in Medicine
Research on Sexual Harassment in the Church
Christian Perspective on Human Sexuality Course: Syllabi
Contemporary Protestant and Roman Catholic Ethics
Environmental Ethics: Course Information and Materials
Environmental Ethics: Course Bibliographies and Readings
Ethical Issues in the Women's Movement
Ethics and Family Life Course: Syllabi
Ethics and Family Life Course: Notes
Ethics and Family Life Course: Bibliographies and Readings
Independent study courses on Mercy, Roman Catholic Dissent
Methods and Sources in Christian Ethics; Natural Law and Christian Ethics; Psychology and Ethics of Commitment
Natural Law and Christian Ethics Reading Course

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This file includes syllabi and a student paper written by Tracy G. Gipson.

did.unittitle: Theological Ethics; Theological Sampler; Truth and Dissent

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This file includes syllabi, reading list, notes, and student papers by James (Jim) Tuite (multiple), Susan J. McCone, J. Patout Burns, S.J., and William Danaher.

did.unittitle: Women's Movement Course: Syllabi and Course Materials
Women's Movement Course: Course Bibliographies and Readings
Women's Movement Course: Session 4 Issues of Sex and Sexuality
Women's Movement Course: Session 5 Lesbian Relations
Women's Movement Course: Sessions 6 & 7 Feminist Ethics

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes article published in the Yale Bulletin about the course entitled, "Project Boosts Interdisciplinary Debate about Bioethical Issues" (2000); Outline and notes regarding the Yale Bioethics Project (2003); Materials from 2005 project "Ethics, Bioethics and Medical Ethics;" and related course materials associated with the interdisciplinary collaborative project.

did.unittitle: Miscellaneous bibliographies, including on Abortion, The Use of the Bible in Christian Ethics, Feminism and Science
ACOG Ethical Committee: Ethical Dimensions of Informed Consent Opinion

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: American College of Obstetricians and Gynecologists (ACOG) Committee on Ethics Opinion Publication Number 108. Several drafts from October 1991 (originally 'Feminist Dimensions of Informed Consent' and final publication, 'Ethical Dimensions of Informed Consent'.

did.unittitle: National Bioethics Advisory Commission (NBAC) on Stem Cell Research

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes agenda and correspondence in regards to the 30th NBAC meeting. Also in this file are draft copies and the final version of the NBAC Ethical Issues In Human Stem Cell Research Religious Perspectives Volume III (Rockland, MD: June 2000). Other testimonies and articles included, as well as notes by Margaret Farley in reference to her testimonial contribution. Although the materials in this file range from early 1999 to June 2000, the National Bioethics Advisory Commission held a meeting on May 7, 1999 and several of the talks presented on this day are included. Later materials in this file are in regard to some of the subjects addressed at this meeting, and used to draft the June 2000 NBAC journal publication.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: American College of Obstetricians and Gynecologists (ACOG) Committee on Ethics Opinion Publication Number 138 (04/1994), "Pre-embryo Research: History, Scientific Background, and Ethical Considerations," authored by Margaret Farley on behalf of the committee. Other materials include correspondence regarding the committee meeting and subject matter, as well as numerous articles and published opinions. The materials also include articles and papers written about the subject after the April 1994 committee opinion was published.

did.unittitle: ACOG and ASRM Ethical Committees: Ethics of Sex Selection

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: American College of Obstetricians and Gynecologists (ACOG) Committee on Ethics Opinion Publication Number 177 (11/1996), "Sex Selection." Other materials include articles, opinions, newspaper selection, and correspondence relevant to the topic.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: ACOG Committee on Ethics opinion Number 55 (10/1987), "Patient Choice: Maternal-Fetal Conflict," and draft of opinion entitled, "Counseling and Consent for Testing: Physicians' Responsibilities." Variety materials including articles, opinions, and correspondence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Research materials, current event articles, and book reviews spanning a decade concerning the subjects of in-vitro fertilization, sperm donation, multiple fetuses, and the legal rights of surrogates and sperm/egg donors.

did.unittitle: Yale Ethics of Stem Cell Research Working Group: Stem-Cell Research

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Communication and research materials for the Yale Ethics Committee on stem cell research

did.unittitle: Yale New Haven Hospital Bioethics Committee

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mission statement and Administrative Policy and Procedure Manual (two versions) of the Bioethics Committee of Yale New Haven Hospital written by Margaret Farley

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This file includes papers given and research in reference to this meeting of the National Bioethics Advisory Commission regarding the theme of Stem Cell Research and Therapy: A Judeo-Biblical Perspective. These papers include: The Ethics of the Eighth Day: Jewish Bioethics and Genetic Medicine by Laurie Zoloth, Stem Cell Research by Rabbi Elliot N. Dorff, Remarks on Human Embryonic Stem Cell Research by Ronald Cole-Turner, An Eastern Orthodox View of Embryonic Stem Cell Research by Rev. Demetrius Demopoulos, and Remarks on Human Embryonic Stem-Cell Research by Gilbert Meilaender.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This committee opinion addresses embryonic stem cells for stem cell research and the ethics of donating embryonic stem cells. An undated draft of this opinion is included, alongside correspondence and other research materials.

did.unittitle: AACSS: All-Africa Conference: Sister to Sister / Women's Initiative Consultation: Project on Gender, Faith, and Responses to HIV/AIDS in Africa
Context for Renunciation: A Study of the Hindu Tradition of Sannyasa, Its Relevant Past and Future

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Research materials and bound draft included.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes multiple book reviews done by Farley regarding the subject of Bioethics.

did.unittitle: Dissertation – A Study in the Ethics of Commitment Within the Context of Theories of Human Love and Temporality

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Prepared for League of Women Voters: Perspectives on Poverty.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Published in Reflections.

did.unittitle: Proceedings of the Thirtieth Annual Convention

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Published in The Catholic Theological Society of America, edited by Luke Salm, F.S.C.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Co-published with Doris Gottemoeller in Volume 34 in Review for Religious.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Published in Network Quarterly, Volume III, Number 4.

did.unittitle: New Patterns of Relationship: Beginnings of a Moral Revolution

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Published in Theological Studies.

did.unittitle: Moral Imperatives for the Ordination of Women
Sources of Sexual Inequality in the History of Christian Thought

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Published in The Journal of Religion, Volume 56.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Published version of a sermon at the Ordination of Marie M. Fortune

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Published in National Catholic Reporter.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Request, Guidelines, and Copy of Farley's contribution to the volume-- 'Sexism'.

did.unittitle: Justice; Integrity and Law; What Needs Must be Ministered to Today?
Discrimination or Equality? The Old Order or The New?

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This is chapter 46 in a volume, under a section called, 'Commentaries.'

did.unittitle: The Church as Christ Living in the World: What Needs Must Be Ministered to Today?

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A chapter in a volume entitled, "Ministering in a Servant Church," edited by Francis A. Eigo and Silvio E. Fittipaldi.

did.unittitle: Fragments for an Ethic of Commitment in Thomas Aquinas

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Published in The Journal of Religion, Volume 58.

did.unittitle: Sexual Ethics; draft review of 'Protestant and Roman Catholic Ethics' by James M. Gustafson

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Published as an entry in the Encyclopedia of Bioethics edited by Warren T. Reich, Volume 4.

did.unittitle: Beyond the Formal Principle: A Reply to Ramsey and Saliers; draft review of 'The Future Partnership' by Letty M. Russell
Book Review of 'Protestant and Roman Catholic Ethics: Prospects for Rapprochement' by James M. Gustafson

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Book review published in Theology Today, Volume XXXVI, Number 2.

did.unittitle: Book Review of 'The Future of Partnership' by Letty M. Russell
Methodology and Ethics; Testing the Vision

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Published in volume 9, number 1 of NETWORK: A Catholic Social Justice Lobby, as part of the 96th congress. Co-written by Francine Cardmam.

did.unittitle: Book Review of, 'The Theory of Morality,' by Alan Donagan

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Published in Religious Studies Review, volume 7, number 3.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Published in Reflections.

did.unittitle: Weep for Yourselves and for Your Children

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Published in Criterion.

did.unittitle: Power and Powerlessness: A Case in Point

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Published as part of a collection of essays entitled, "Panel: Women and Power in the Church."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Published as part of a collection entitled, " A Challenge to Love."

did.unittitle: Obligating Features of Personhood; Feminist Ethics in the Christian Ethics Curriculum

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Article published in Horizons: The Journal of the College of Theology Society, published at Villanova University, as part of the Fall 1984 issue. Correspondence and two drafts included.

did.unittitle: The Church and The Family: An Ethical Task

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Published in Horizons, volume 10, number 1.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: From A Challenge to Love: Gay and Lesbian Catholics in the Church edited by Robert Nugent (NY: Crossroad Press, 1983)

did.unittitle: Canonical Regulation of Women's Religious Communities; When is Termination of Pregnancy During the Third Trimester Morally Justifiable?

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes copies of a draft, two final copies, a copyright request, and feedback regarding the paper. Co-written by F.A. Chervenak, L. Walters, J.C. Hobbins, and M.J. Mahoney.

did.unittitle: Can Faith Survive Injustice? A Question for Women in the Church

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two drafts included, one is annotated.

did.unittitle: Creative Teaching: Feminist Ethics in The Christian Ethics Curriculum

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Published in Horizons, volume 11, number 2.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Published as chapter 19 in an edited volume.

did.unittitle: Feminist Consciousness and the Interpretation of Scripture

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Published as chapter 3 in an edited volume, "Feminist Interpretation of the Bible."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Published as an entry contribution to The Westminster Dictionary of Christian Ethics, edited by James F. Childress and John MacQuarrie.

did.unittitle: From Moral Insight to Moral Choice: The Problem of Conflicting Obligations
Moral Discourse in the Public Arena

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Published in a volume entitled, "Vatican Authority and American Catholic Dissent: The Curran Case and Its Consequences," edited by William W. May.

did.unittitle: Moral Discussions in the Public Arena

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This is an article published for Crossroads in a volume on Authority and Dissent in 1987 originally. Research materials and copy included.

did.unittitle: Author's Response: Reviews of Personal Commitments; survey of recent titles in Christian ethics

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Published in Horizons, volume 15, number 1. This is a response to four reviews given in this edition of Farley's work entitled, "Personal Commitments: Beginning, Keeping, Changing."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This is an excerpt from the address Farley gave to the 1989 graduates at the 66th commencement of Weston School of Theology on May 27, published in "News from Weston: A National Jesuit Theological Center," volume IX, Number 4.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Chapter 11 in a volume of Essays in Honor of Richard A. McCormick called, "Moral Theology: Challenged for the Future," edited by Charles E. Curran.

did.unittitle: Divorce and Remarriage: A Moral Perspective

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Chapter 6 of a volume entitled, "Divorce and Remarriage: Religious and Psychological Perspectives," edited by William P. Roberts.

did.unittitle: A New Form of Communion: Feminism and the Chinese Church

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Published in volume 164, number 6, of AMERICA.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A response to Martin Marty's "analysis of the challenges facing this nation on the 200th anniversary of the Bill of Rights." Published in Religious Education number 86.

did.unittitle: A New Form of Communion: Feminism and the Chinese Church

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence, notes, and drafts of the paper. Original folder includes notes written on it.

did.unittitle: Christian Faith and the Experience of Women

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Published in Akebono Magazine. Includes correspondence and the brief.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Published in The Living Pulpit, volume 1, number 2.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This is an address presented to the Baltimore Regional Community Chapter on January 24, 1992, published in The MAST Journal, volume 2, number 3.

did.unittitle: The Concept of Commitment as Applied to Questions of Marriage and Divorce

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Published in CLSA PROCEEDINGS, volume 54.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Published as chapter 7 in an unknown volume.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Article published in "The Living Pulpit," and a letter to students concerning a free year-long subscription to the journal. Also includes information regarding the magazine, and an original draft.

did.unittitle: A Feminist Version of Respect for Persons

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Published in Journal of Feminist Studies in Religion, volume 9, numbers 1-2, A Special Issue in Honor of Beverly Wildung Harrison.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Annotated copy of a contribution to an unknown encyclopedia.

did.unittitle: Embodiment, Morality, and Medicine Preface and Paperwork

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contract and royalty paperwork for, "Embodiment, Morality, and Medicine," co-edited by Lisa Cahill. Draft of preface included.

did.unittitle: Dictionary of Feminist Theologies - 'Ethics and Moral Theology,' and 'Relationships'

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence and notes regarding entries contracted to M. Farley for the Dictionary of Feminist Theologies including 'Ethics and moral theology' and 'Relationships.' Also includes master list of terms and contracted contributors.

did.unittitle: How Shall We Love in a Postmodern World?

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Published in The Annual of the Society of Christian Ethics as the Presidential Address.

did.unittitle: Pre-Embryo Research: History, Scientific Background, and Ethical Considerations; Ideology of the Family

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Published in the ACOG committee opinion from the Committee on Ethics. A copy is also included in the series Committee Materials.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Published as an entry in the Encyclopedia of Bioethics edited by Warren T. Reich, Volume 5.

did.unittitle: Issues in Contemporary Christian Ethics: The Choice of Death in a Medical Context

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Published version of a lecture given at The Santa Clara Lectures, volume 1, number 3.

did.unittitle: North American Bioethics: A Feminist Critique (1/2)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Article published in "Meta Medical Ethics," edited by M.A. Grodin. Several drafts included, alongside publishing correspondence. Typed draft included also.

did.unittitle: North American Bioethics: A Feminist Critique (2/2)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contribution to an encyclopedia called, "Ethics and Moral Theologies."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

did.unittitle: Afterword and Retrospective: The Life and Writings of Christopher F. Mooney, S.J.

did.unittitle: Research Cloning and Stem Cell Research

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

did.unittitle: Beyond the Formal Principle: A Reply to Ramsey and Saliers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Subjects: Liturgy, Symbol, Ethics. Includes a copy of the published paper and research materials.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This is a chapter in an unidentified volume regarding Roman Catholicism and same-sex relationships.

scopecontent.p: Published in the Daily News.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

did.unittitle: Environmental Ethics: "Religious Meanings for Nature and Humanity."

scopecontent.p: Research materials and reading lists. Includes correspondence with Judith A. Dwyer at Weston School of Theology.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a paper entitled, "Ethical and Religious Responses to Intellectual Disabilities," a contract for the publication of an article on the subject in The Journal of Religion in Disability and Rehabilitation, and a program for a symposium held at Yale called, "Mental Retardation: Ethical, Theological, and Pastoral Issues."

did.unittitle: Hastings Center AIDS Project- Christopher F. Mooney

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: In the wake of the death of Christopher F. Mooney, professor of religious studies at Fairfield University, Margaret Farley was part of the team to finish and publish his final work posthumously. Though there is little information about this in the file itself, this work is affiliated with the Hastings Center Scholars program for bioethical research regarding theology, cosmology, and science with special interest in AIDS research.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A copy of the English language publication of Noticias Aliades, volume 15, issue 40.

did.unittitle: Lining Up on the Law of the Sea: Reagan Opts for Anarchy

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Published in Christianity and Crisis by Theophane Mathias.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bibliography for a reading course collected by William J. Damrow.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Dissertation proposal submitted by Richard T. Stith III entitled, "A Theory of Respect."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a paper written by Farley entitled, "Toward an Ethical Critique of Contemporary Revolution," and other materials.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Course plan instructed by Tracy and Gustafson, along with notes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes multiple news articles.

did.unittitle: South African Ethical Investment by Yale

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes articles published by Yale University in regards to Ethical Investment Policy in South Africa.
